content: Clinical management of tolerance: opioid rotation when tolerance limits analgesia (incomplete cross-tolerance allows dose reduction); drug holidays to restore sensitivity (nitrates require 10-12 hour nitrate-free interval); combination therapy to reduce individual drug tolerance development (cancer pain: opioids + NSAIDs + adjuvants); scheduled dosing rather than PRN to maintain steady levels and minimize tolerance. Benzodiazepine tolerance: limit duration to 2-4 weeks for anxiety/insomnia; if long-term needed, consider intermittent dosing or switch to non-benzodiazepine alternatives. Stimulant tolerance in ADHD: drug holidays may help but risk symptom recurrence; consider dose adjustment or alternative agents. Preventing tolerance: use lowest effective dose; avoid unnecessary dose escalation; address underlying causes rather than escalating symptomatic treatment.
